name: Leif Erikson
description: Leif Erikson, son of Erik the Red, was a Norse explorer from Iceland who is credited as the first European to set foot on continental North America, predating Christopher Columbus by nearly five centuries. Born around 970 AD, he grew up in Greenland after his father was exiled from Iceland. Around the year 1000 AD, while sailing from Norway back to Greenland, Leif was blown off course and landed on a new land he called Vinland, known today to be part of North America, possibly Newfoundland. He explored the region, establishing a temporary settlement before returning to Greenland with timber and grapes. While his settlement was short-lived, Leif's voyage marked a significant moment in history, demonstrating Norse seafaring prowess and their early connection to the New World. Leif inherited his father's adventurous spirit, but also possessed a strong sense of justice and leadership, exemplified by his rescue of shipwrecked sailors. His legacy lives on in sagas and historical accounts as a daring explorer and a pivotal figure in Norse history.
subtitle: Norse Explorer of North America
achievements: First European to land in North America
Established a Norse settlement in North America

keyMyths.title: Discovery of Vinland
keyMyths.description: Leif Erikson, blown off course, landed on a new land he named Vinland, rich in grapes and timber. He explored the area and established a temporary camp before returning to Greenland.
keyMyths.source: The Saga of Erik the Red

keyMyths.title: Rescue of Shipwrecked Sailors
keyMyths.description: During a voyage, Leif encountered shipwrecked sailors and rescued them, demonstrating his compassion and earning him respect. This act highlights his character beyond exploration.
keyMyths.source: The Saga of Erik the Red

keyMyths.title: Leif's Inheritance
keyMyths.description: Leif inherited his father Erik the Red's adventurous nature, but proved a more level-headed leader. He balanced exploration with responsibility, ensuring the safety of his crew.
keyMyths.source: The Saga of Erik the Red

sources.author: Anonymous
sources.work: The Saga of Erik the Red
sources.description: A primary source detailing Leif Erikson's voyages and the Norse exploration of Vinland.
sources.text: A primary source detailing Leif Erikson's voyages and the Norse exploration of Vinland.

sources.description: Another saga offering accounts of the Norse exploration of North America, including Leif Erikson's role.
